description: US-04 Look Up a Customer by Card Number — The counter screen: one input, one unmissable verdict, everything needed to decide. Includes the portions/price display stories moved here from US-07, since they need this screen to exist.

userStories.title: evaluateAtCounter — the counter verdict rule
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function returning exactly one verdict for a customer at the counter, so the decision is testable in milliseconds and cannot drift between screens. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/counterVerdict.ts exports evaluateAtCounter(input) returning a discriminated union

Verdict cases are exactly: NOT_FOUND, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED (carries reason), WRONG_GROUP (carries customer group and today's colour), OUTDATED_CARD (carries presented and current card number), ALREADY_SERVED_TODAY, CLEAR_TO_SERVE, CLEAR_TO_SERVE_CERTIFICATE_EXPIRED (carries expiry date and reminder count)
Precedence is NOT_FOUND, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED, WRONG_GROUP, OUTDATED_CARD, certificate check, CLEAR_TO_SERVE — and every adjacent ordering pair has its own test
An expired certificate never produces a blocking verdict
today and weekColour are parameters — no clock and no I/O in the module
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: lookupCustomer use case
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a read-only use case that resolves a typed number to a customer and returns the verdict plus everything the counter screen shows. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.2
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts a raw string and parses it as a card number (50k3) or a bare customer number (50)
A bare customer number resolves to the active holder of that slot; when only archived customers ever held it, the verdict is ARCHIVED for the most recent holder
A card index lower than the holder's current index yields OUTDATED_CARD; an unassigned customer number yields NOT_FOUND
The result carries name, customer number, group, counts, portions, priceCents, certificate expiry, status, reminder count, notes and current card number
Counts, portions and price are computed at read time — never read from stored columns
Test asserts the fake repository received no mutations for every verdict branch
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock for all verdict branches
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Counter lookup queries and indexes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the lookup to resolve reassigned slots correctly and stay instant. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.3
acceptanceCriteria: findByCustomerNumber(n) returns the active holder, plus the most recent archived holder when there is no active one
Indexes exist on Customer.customerNumber and Customer.status
Household members, certificate and cards are loaded in one query — no N+1 at the counter
Integration test with an archived and an active customer both holding number 50 resolves correctly
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/ if the schema changed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The query and the indexes already landed with US-002; this story added the proof. One deviation from the wording: 'loaded in one query' is not reachable on Prisma's SQLite provider, which has no join strategy (relationLoadStrategy: 'join' is Postgres/MySQL only). A lookup is four fixed statements — the customer plus one per relation — and the test pins the invariant that actually matters, that the count does not grow with household size. No migration: the schema was already correct.

userStories.title: Counter lookup screen on /ausgabe
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to type a number and see one clear answer, so I can serve a queue quickly. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.4
acceptanceCriteria: /ausgabe shows the week-colour banner and a single auto-focused number input; Enter performs the lookup and the input clears and re-focuses
The verdict renders as a full-width banner with a distinct colour AND an explicit German sentence and icon — never colour alone

Wrong group renders the send-away sentence naming both colours; outdated card names the presented and the current card number; blocked shows the reason verbatim; archived states not eligible; expired certificate states serve-and-remind; otherwise clear to serve
Below the verdict: name, customer number, group, counts, portions, price, certificate expiry, status, reminder count and notes are all visible without further clicks
An unknown number renders 'Nummer nicht gefunden' and keeps the input focused
The UI switch over the verdict union is exhaustive with a never-check in the default branch
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified in a real browser with a throwaway Playwright spec (no dev-browser MCP tools in this environment): CLEAR_TO_SERVE, WRONG_GROUP, CLEAR_TO_SERVE_CERTIFICATE_EXPIRED, NOT_FOUND and a nonsense entry all render, and the input is empty and focused again after Enter. The permanent spec is US-005. The serve action (PRD §US-04.4, last bullet) is not here: US-05 has not been built, so there is nothing to render.

userStories.title: E2E: every verdict at the counter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec covering each counter verdict and proving read-only behaviour. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec seeds one customer per verdict and asserts the banner text for each
Spec asserts that looking up a wrong-group customer and an outdated card leaves the database unchanged: no distribution record, no reminder, no status change
Spec asserts an unknown number shows the not-found message rather than an empty page
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Display portions and price across the customer screens
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want portions and price shown wherever I look at a customer, so I hand out the right amount without arithmetic. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.4
acceptanceCriteria: The counter screen, the card view and the customer detail page all show portions and price
Money renders as German-formatted euro (e.g. 2,50 €) via formatEuros from src/domain/money.ts
Each screen states these are the standard values; there is no control to adjust them
Money renders as German-formatted euro via formatEuros, derived from the counts and the settings in force
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: portions and price follow the household
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the allowance is derived, not stored. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec asserts a customer with 2 grown-ups and 1 child shows the seeded portions and price
Spec asserts adding a household member changes the portions and the price on reload
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
